body.title: [sprint] Halt-until-sorted: refuse to start work / prove on a dirty tree
body.description:
No-hidden-state principle (D-proof-on-close-2026-06-20): uncommitted work is deferred/hidden state (git's hollow build); it must be sorted immediately (committed or reverted), never stashed or deferred. A dirty tree HALTS work until sorted, at two points: (a) start of a unit of work, (b) proof/close time (the proof_emitter clean-tree guard, already shipped, is this half). Workflow-halt (CC stops, surfaces, sorts) — NOT the device-bus HALT/priority signal. Scope = work-boundaries + proof-time, NOT continuous (avoid the Igor email-panic over-eager-urgency failure).

**Affected files:** TBD — work-start check in the sprint/ticket skills + CC behavior; proposed CLAUDE.md Structural rule ('don't start a unit of work on a dirty tree; sort it first'). CLAUDE.md is HIGH-inertia — rule TEXT needs Akien approval before this builds.
**Design rules:** prove-what's-load-bearing; do-it-right-not-deprecate-in-place
**Scope boundary:** IN — the work-start halt check + (approved) CLAUDE.md rule; OUT — bus-HALT signal, continuous/global enforcement
**Test plan:** a work-start check that detects `git status --porcelain` non-empty and halts with a clear 'commit or revert first' message; test asserts halt fires on dirty + passes on clean. CLAUDE.md rule text reviewed by Akien before merge.
